major: History of Art and Visual Culture Minor

courses.title: Colonial Histories and Legacies: Africa, Oceania, and the Indigenous Americas
History of Art: Africa, Oceania, and Indigenous North America

notes: UC Santa Cruz students may choose to complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and allowable credits. While a minor will appear on a student's official transcript, it will not be listed on their diploma. Pursuing a minor is a personal decision that should align with a student's educational and career goals. Students do not apply for minors when submitting their application to UC Santa Cruz. After enrolling, transfer students interested in pursuing a minor should contact the department offering the minor for guidance.

The History of Art and Visual Culture (HAVC) program at UC Santa Cruz welcomes transfer students. Although not required for admission, students are encouraged to complete up to three of their lower-division HAVC minor requirements prior to transfer, each selected from a different geographic region: Africa and its Diaspora Asia and its Diaspora Europe and the Americas Mediterranean Native Americas Oceania and its Diaspora HAVC 80 – Colonial Histories and Legacies: Africa, Oceania, and the Indigenous Americas* *HAVC 80, or an articulated equivalent, may be used to fulfill a lower-division requirement for one of the following geographic regions: Africa, Native Americas, or Oceania. In the “Preparation for the Minor” section below, you will find lists of courses approved to fulfill these requirements. Petitioning Courses: Transfer students who have completed courses they believe are applicable to the geographic regions listed above, but are not listed in the "Recommended Preparation" section below, may petition to have these courses counted toward their HAVC minor requirements. Students should consult the Transfer Credit Petition Process instructions on the HAVC Department website . Please visit the History of Art and Visual Culture website to learn more about this minor.
